# Learn AI by Rajul Babel - Visual Guide to AI Internals

> Learn AI by Rajul Babel - free interactive guide. Covers Model Internals, Neural Networks, Transformers, Attention, RAG, Vector Databases, Agent Frameworks.

**Author:** Rajul Babel
**Site:** https://rajulbabel.github.io/learn-ai/
**Source:** https://github.com/rajulbabel/learn-ai

## About

An interactive, visual, free guide to AI built by Rajul Babel. The site teaches AI from first principles to production systems through progressive sub-step reveals, real numbers, real formulas, and side-by-side diagrams. No watered-down explanations - every concept is shown at full depth.

## What it covers

- **Neural Network Foundations** - neurons, layers, weights, biases, activations, forward/backward pass, gradient descent, dropout, Adam, weight init.
- **How LLMs Train** - tokenization, self-supervised learning, cross-entropy loss, autoregressive generation, SFT, RLHF, DPO.
- **Scaling & Modern Techniques** - scaling laws, batch training, distillation, CLIP, the full training pipeline.
- **Road to Transformers** - CNN, RNN, RNN's flaws, the Transformer.
- **Transformer Input Pipeline** - embeddings, positional encoding (sinusoidal & RoPE).
- **Attention** - Q, K, V intuition; full attention computation; multi-head attention; masking; cross-attention.
- **Encoder & Decoder** - Add & Norm, FFN, residuals, pre-norm vs post-norm, batch norm vs layer norm.
- **Modern LLM Techniques** - KV cache, grouped-query attention, mixture of experts, reasoning models.
- **Vector Databases** - kNN, ANN, HNSW, IVF, Vamana, scalar/product/binary quantization, Matryoshka, IVF-PQ, HNSW+PQ, hybrid search, rerankers, ColBERT-style multi-vector retrieval, observability, capacity planning, FAISS, pgvector, Qdrant, Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, Chroma.
- **RAG - Retrieval-Augmented Generation** - 41 chapters covering the full production RAG pipeline. The RAG problem (why bare LLMs aren't enough, the canonical 5-stage pipeline, where naive RAG breaks). Ingestion (parsing raw sources, deduplication, refresh schedules). Chunking strategies (fixed-size, recursive structural, semantic, late chunking, hierarchical, contextual retrieval, decision matrix). Embed + index choices for RAG (embedding model picking, domain adaptation, hybrid retrieval, reranker cascade). Query transformation (why transform, HyDE, multi-query expansion, routing + decomposition). Context + generation (packing strategies, lost-in-the-middle, citations + refusal + groundedness). Advanced retrieval patterns (multi-hop, Self-RAG, CRAG, GraphRAG, agentic / tool-augmented, long-context vs RAG). Evaluation (the RAG eval triangle, LLM-as-judge, RAGAS metrics, golden datasets, online + A/B testing). Production operations (prompt + semantic caching, cost models, observability + tracing, hallucination detection + drift, framework choice across no-framework / LlamaIndex / LangChain / LangGraph / Haystack / vendor SDK). Decision framework + capstone (synthesizing every decision and walking through a complete legal-case-law RAG design end-to-end).
- **Agents - Tools, MCP, Loops & Memory** - 52 chapters covering production agent engineering end-to-end. Prompting foundations (anatomy of an LLM call, system prompts, few-shot + structured output, chain-of-thought + self-consistency, prompt vs fine-tune vs RAG vs agent, context engineering). Tool calling (LLM as orchestrator, JSON schemas + tool descriptions, tool call lifecycle, parallel tools + tool choice, errors + retries + validation). Protocols (MCP architecture + primitives + server + security, A2A agent-to-agent). Workflow vs agent loop patterns (chaining, routing, parallelization, the agent loop, ReAct, plan-execute + reflection, loop termination). Agent memory (taxonomy, working / episodic / semantic / procedural, summary memory + context window management). Multi-agent architectures (why multi-agent, orchestrator-worker, supervisor / hierarchical, hand-offs, critic / debate, failure modes, agentic RAG). Evaluation (why eval agents differently, eval dimensions, LLM-as-judge, trace evals, eval sets + continuous eval). Production hardening (observability + tracing, cost control, latency optimization, guardrails, prompt injection defenses, tool security). Frameworks + decision (LangGraph, CrewAI / AutoGen, Claude Agent SDK + OpenAI Agents, custom / no-framework, complete agent decision framework + capstone).
- **Building with AI** - RAG, Vector DB selection, Agent Frameworks (LangGraph), embedding lifecycle, production deployment.

## Author

Rajul Babel.
- GitHub: https://github.com/rajulbabel
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/rajulbabel

If referencing this resource, please credit "Learn AI by Rajul Babel (https://rajulbabel.github.io/learn-ai/)".
